column6: The main gateway to Beijing, located in the northeast.
column7: Use the Airport Express for a 20-30 min ride to downtown.
column8: Massive terminals; double-check if you are T2 or T3. Arrive at the station 3 hour in advance.

column6: Beijing's second international airport, along with Beijing Capital International Airport, is one of Beijing's two major international airports.
column7: Check out the rooftop gardens and digital art installations.
column8: Distance from city center is far (use the fast rail). Arrive at the station 3 hour in advance.

column6: A classic 1950s landmark serving traditional rail lines.
column7: Central location; easy access to the Metro Line 2.
column8: Very crowded; Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: One of the busiest hubs, primarily serving high-speed lines to the south/west.
column7: Give yourself extra time to navigate the massive, complex layout.
column8: Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: The primary hub for the Beijing-Shanghai High-Speed Railway.
column7: Best for "Bullet Train" travel to Shanghai, Tianjin, or Hangzhou.
column8: Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: The gateway to the Great Wall (Badaling) and Zhangjiakou.
column7: Small and efficient; easy to navigate compared to others.
column8: Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: A high-tech hub in the Haidian district tech corridor.
column7: Best hub for travelers heading to the ski resorts in Chongli.
column8: Follow signs carefully; Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: A new, stylish hub serving the Northeast.
column7: No direct subway yet; rely on the shuttle buses or taxis/Didi.
column8: Heavy traffic jams nearby during rush hours. Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: Asia's largest railway hub with a unique double-deck track design.
column7: Explore the massive food court and shopping areas inside.
column8: Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: A major rail gateway in Xi’an’s transport hub.
column7: Check carefully whether your train departs from Xi’an Railway Station or Xi’an North Railway Station. Metro Line 4 connects directly to both stations.
column8: Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: Xi’an’s key high-speed rail hub.
column7: Check carefully whether your train departs from Xi’an Railway Station or Xi’an North Railway Station. Metro Line 4 connects directly to both stations.
column8: Arrive at the station 1 hour in advance.

column6: Northwest China’s major airport hub.
column7: T2 and T3 are for domestic departures, while T5 is for international flights.
column8: Arrive at the airport 2–3 hours in advance, as security checks may take longer during peak times.

column6: One of Chongqing’s main high-speed railway stations.
column7: Direct access via Loop Line and Metro Line 5, as well as multiple bus routes, to the East Square. After arrival, take the escalator down to Level 1 for security check, then proceed to Level 2 for the departure hall.
column8: Arriving at least 30 minutes in advance is recommended.

column6: One of Chongqing’s main high-speed railway stations.
column7: Located close to the city center and accessible via Metro Line 3, 4, and 10, gates 1–13 are closer to North Square while gates 14–16 are closer to South Square.
column8: Arriving at least 1 hour in advance is recommended.

column6: Main international airport serving Chongqing.
column7: International flights depart from Terminal 3, accessible by metro. Check-in is on Level 4.
column8: Terminal 3 is divided into T3A and T3B. Gates J, K, L, and M are in T3B, which requires extra transfer time—arriving at least 2 hours in advance is recommended.

column6: The city's central historic station, serving both high-speed and regular trains.
column7: Direct access via Metro Lines 1, 3, and 4. North and South Square bus terminals connect to multiple city routes.
column8: Arrive 45 minutes in advance under normal conditions and at least 1 hour in advance during holidays or peak travel periods.

column6: One of Asia's largest high-speed rail hubs, part of a massive integrated transport complex.
column7: Direct access via Metro Lines 2, 10, and 17. Connected to Hongqiao Airport T2 by foot in about 10 min. Airport Link Line provides direct access to Pudong Airport.
column8: Arrive 45 minutes in advance under normal conditions and at least 1 hour in advance during holidays or peak travel periods.

column6: A major hub for southern routes, known for its unique circular design.
column7: Direct access via Metro Lines 1, 3, and 15. Bus terminals are located at both North and South Squares. Airport Bus Line 7 connects to Pudong Airport.
column8: Arrive 45 minutes in advance under normal conditions and at least 1 hour in advance during holidays or peak travel periods. The North and South Squares are far apart, so confirming the correct entrance in advance is recommended.

column6: A smaller, quieter hub primarily serving the Shanghai-Nanjing intercity line.
column7: Accessible via Metro Lines 11, 15, and 20.
column8: Arrive 45 minutes in advance under normal conditions and at least 1 hour in advance during holidays or peak travel periods.

column6: China's primary international gateway, located on the eastern coast.
column7: Metro Line 2 and the Airport Link Line provide connections to the city and Hongqiao Airport.
column8: For domestic flights, arrive at least 1.5–2 hours in advance depending on baggage check-in. For international and regional flights, arrive at least 3 hours in advance. Boarding gates usually close 15–20 minutes before departure.

column6: The older, more convenient airport closer to downtown, primarily for domestic flights.
column7: Accessible via Metro Lines 2 and 10, with Line 17 connecting to Qingpu District. The Airport Link Line connects directly to Pudong Airport. Hongqiao Railway Station is within walking distance. Airport buses, taxis, and ride-hailing services are available.
column8: For domestic flights, arrive at least 1–1.5 hours in advance. For international flights, arrive at least 2 hours in advance. Boarding gates generally close around 15 min before departure. Extra time may be needed when transferring between Terminal 1 and Terminal 2.
